channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: As women seek open congressional seats in Maryland, reproductive rights are front and center
item.description: In an election where the future of reproductive rights is on the ballot in Maryland and elsewhere across the country, the state’s all-male congressional delegation stands to gain an influx of women.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Uphill battles that put abortion rights on ballots are unlikely to end even if the measures pass
item.description: Voters in nine states are deciding next month whether to add the right to abortion to their constitutions.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to pick ‘best’ Republicans for 2nd term Cabinet: Lutnick
item.description: Donald Trump is in the unusual position of not being backed by a number of members of his previous Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Harris Fox News interview draws sharp reactions on both sides
item.description: “If she wins, it will be because of that answer … which is ‘I’m not Joe Biden,’” columnist Chris Cillizza noted.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked whether Trump lost 2020 election
item.description: In the months since he became Donald Trump’s running mate, Ohio Sen. JD Vance has repeatedly deflected questions about whether the Republican presidential nominee lost the 2020 election, saying he was focused on the future.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Some Kern County voters say ballots are missing; election officials say it's not a concern
item.description: Once your ballot arrives in the mail, you can fill it out and then send it back by mail or drop it off at a drop box.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bakersfield Chamber of Commerce, DA Zimmer urge support for Prop 36
item.description: "Californians, whether they be independents, Democrats, or Republicans have had enough," said Kern County District Attorney Cynthia Zimmer.
item.dc:creator: Connor Dore, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas lawmakers signal push to require proof of citizenship from voters
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott gave an inflated number of noncitizens removed from the state’s voter rolls, officials acknowledged, confirming findings by ProPublica, The Texas Tribune, and Votebeat.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By James Barragán, The Texas Tribune, Lexi Churchill, The Texas Tribune And Propublica, Natalia Contreras, Votebeat And The Texas Tribune, And Vianna Davila, The Texas Tribune And Propublica, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris campaign calls plagiarism claims a partisan attack. Expert says it was 'sloppy writing'
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris' campaign is dismissing accusations that she and a co-author plagiarized parts of a 2009 book on the U.S. criminal justice system as a desperate attempt by “rightwing operatives” to distract voters.
item.dc:creator: RICHARD LARDNER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida elections officials seek flexibility on voting rules after hurricanes
item.description: Florida's recent hurricanes could make it difficult for voters in affected areas to cast their ballots in the upcoming election unless Gov. Ron DeSantis waives and modifies some rules, county election supervisors said in a letter to state officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris and Trump offer new details about policies and strategy in dueling interviews
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the presidential race moves into its final weeks, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump embarked on an interview blitz that offered new details about their policy priorities and their political strategies.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, COLLEEN LONG and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris interview with Fox News showcases a change in strategy for Democrats with network
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Kamala Harris ' interview with Fox News Channel's Bret Baier on Wednesday is the latest indication that Democrats during this campaign are increasingly willing to engage with a network well-stocked with supporters of opponent Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is California's Proposition 5 on votes for bond measures?
item.description: Nearly every election cycle, Californians vote on a measures to allow local governments to borrow money in the form of bonds. Under state law, however, it is quite hard for these measures to actually pass — something that could change in November.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden delivers final eulogy during Ethel Kennedy memorial service in DC
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, died last week. She had been hospitalized after suffering a stroke earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Dems target Black voters with new ad as Harris’ support falls short
item.description: Senate Democrats are advertising to Black voters specifically as Vice President Kamala Harris struggles with the voting group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Biden is out campaigning again — but not for her husband anymore. She's pumping up Harris
item.description: CLAWSON, Mich. (AP) — Jill Biden wasted no time after she stepped up to the microphone at a suburban Detroit restaurant. “Now some have come to (the) Detroit area recently and thrown around some insults, but from what I’ve seen this is a vibrant, thriving city,” she said.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is Prop 36? A look into ‘The Homelessness, Drug Addiction, and Theft Reduction Act’
item.description: (KRON) – San Francisco Mayor London Breed, the California District Attorneys Association, and the California State Sheriffs' Association are all in support of Proposition 36, an initiative aimed to reverse a previous proposition that supporters claim exacerbated theft and crime around California.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voters to face climate-related ballot initiatives, such as repealing law to reduce emissions by 95% by 2050
item.description: In 2024, voters in multiple states will decide on key ballot initiatives that will shape their climate and environmental policies for the next several years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Small business disaster loan program is out of money until new funds are approved
item.description: The SBA’s disaster loan program has run out of money, it announced on Tuesday. The agency expects to receive new funding from Congress, and will continue to accept applications in the meantime.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu and Miguel Macias, MPR News

item.title: Harris' Fox interview a bigger risk for Bret Baier: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly discusses the state of the race as Kamala Harris and Donald Trump work to energize key voter groups.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: ISIS-K orchestrated foiled Election Day terror plot: US official
item.description: Afghan native Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, was arrested in early October and accused of planning to target people on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Hennepin County achieves 'functional zero' status in ending veteran homelessness
item.description: The designation from several federal agencies indicates a community has brought down the number of veterans experiencing homelessness to a very low level and continues to sustain the effort.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Senate Intel chair frets about foreign actors spreading hurricane, election misinformation
item.description: Senate Intelligence Committee Chair Mark Warner (D-Va.) on Tuesday indicated that he is concerned foreign actors are spreading misinformation related to the recovery from a pair of recent hurricanes and will be ramping up efforts amid the final election sprint.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump uses interview on economics to promote tariffs and riff on his favorite themes
item.description: Donald Trump has seized on an opening to sound his frequent argument that imposing huge tariffs on foreign goods would amount to an economic elixir — one he claims would raise enormous sums for the government, protect U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: One-quarter of applicants ruled out colleges because of states' political climate: Poll
item.description: Out of that group, about two-thirds decided to exclude certain schools at the beginning of their search, the organization found.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump clashes with Bloomberg editor over tariffs
item.description: “That is going to have a serious effect on the overall economy,” Bloomberg’s top editor, John Micklethwait said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump town hall ends with extended 'musical-fest' while he stands onstage
item.description: At a town hall in Oaks, Pa., on Monday, Trump ended the Q&A portion to ask the audience to remain and listen to music with him.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Jean-Pierre blocking Kirby from White House podium, report says
item.description: Karine Jean-Pierre leads the daily press briefing, while John Kirby often handles questions about foreign affairs.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Geraldo Rivera endorses Harris, says Trump 'unfit'
item.description: NewsNation correspondent-at-large Geraldo Rivera said Monday that he's endorsing Kamala Harris for president.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump edges out Harris with early voters in battleground states
item.description: Former President Trump has a slight edge over Vice President Harris with early voters in battleground states, new polling shows, a promising sign for the Republican in the highly competitive presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez, challenger Joe Kent face off in Washington 3rd District debate
item.description: One of the races that could determine the fate of Congress this election season happens to be the rematch race for Washington's 3rd District, and on Monday night, the candidates will debate on KOIN 6.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: With hunting party, Walz invites moderate male voters into the fold
item.description: Independent voter David Clayton joined Gov. Tim Walz at Saturday’s pheasant opener and says he’s sold on voting for the Harris-Walz ticket. He thinks others may be, too, but aren’t talking about it. “There are many, many MAGA-minded Americans who aren’t really so MAGA-minded.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Megan Burks, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: VP hopefuls make Midwest push as Vance pops in on Minnesota, Walz swings through Wisconsin
item.description: A near-meeting on the tarmac at the Minneapolis-St. Paul airport opens the campaign week for Tim Walz and JD Vance, the vice presidential nominees on their party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Eric Adams is 'working well through the chaos': Gov. Hochul
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul offered support for embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams during the Columbus Day Parade in Manhattan on Monday. "Told him to bring in new blood… he's doing that… working well through the chaos," she told PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11
